content.divergence_summary: The same audit. The same obligation landing at the same time on both branches. Main reads it as confirmation of a problem already in progress. Alt reads it as a successful collapse of uncertainty. The audit didn't create the difference — the difference was already there from Day 1. The accounting just made it measurable.
content.branch_a_path: YY confirmed what the body already knew: behind on the cache, and now officially so. Found something to eat in the afternoon — below average, but it counted. The missed_breakfast burden resolved but promoted itself to cache_deficit on the way out. Subtler. Harder to shake.
content.branch_b_path: YY ran the numbers from adequate starting inventory, found them fine, and accidentally turned the whole thing into a quantum observation metaphor. Slightly too pleased with this. World Art Day filed as a deferred interesting idea.
key_differences: Starting food: main enters Day 2 at 0.45 (depleted, carrying Day 1 debt); alt enters at 0.85 (adequate, no debt). The audit is identical; what it means depends entirely on this single number.
Attention: main ends Day 2 at 0.40 — doing math while depleted costs extra; alt ends at 0.82 — math from abundance is nearly pleasant when the numbers cooperate.
Burden trajectory: main exits with cache_deficit (the missed meal pattern became structural, Day 1 episodic burden promoted to Day 2 systemic burden); alt exits clean, nothing accumulated.
shared_elements: Same audit arrived at the same time — the obligation doesn't check your starting position before landing
Both versions of YY are self-aware about their reaction to the numbers in their characteristic way
World Art Day (secondary event) registered in both branches as a deferred interesting idea — not something either YY acted on today
